quoteFirst: The doctor generally makes you drink a glass
quoteLast: , then take a little walk, then drink another glass, and another little walk and so on until about eight, when you can go to the Swiss bakery and get the zwiebach or twice baked bread, which is handed you in a paper bag, and then you can go to some cafay on the sidewalk and get coffee or tea and boiled eggs and make out your breakfast.

My German Prisons
Being the Experiences of an Officer During Two and a Half Years as a Prisoner of War
author: Gilliland, Horace Gray

quoteFirst: "The attack was renewed by the enemy
quoteTime: about seven o'clock in the morning
quoteLast: , and was bravely resisted by the limited number of troops I could bring into action until eleven o'clock, when orders were given for the army to establish itself on the new line. This it did in good order. The position I abandoned was one that I had held at a disadvantage; and I kept the troops on it as long as I did, only for the purpose of enabling me to hear of the approach of the force under Gen. Sedgwick."
title: The Campaign of Chancellorsville

quoteFirst: We were due to billet in Locre, and there we arrived at
quoteLast: It was frightfully cold, but, after we had seen the two battalions billeted, the military policeman who had been told to turn up and show us to our billets was nowhere to be found, so we wandered on as far as the Convent, staggering and slipping on the snowy ice and blowing on our fingers as we went.
title: The Doings of the Fifteenth Infantry Brigade: August 1914 to March 1915

quoteLast: of the 27th; when the weather moderated.
